schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Kisan Samman Nidhi (PM-Kisan)
schemes.benefit: ₹6,000 per year transferred directly to the bank account in 3 equal installments.

eligibility.land_ownership: Must own cultivable land in their name.

schemes.application_process: Register via the PM-Kisan portal (pmkisan.gov.in) or at the nearest Common Service Centre (CSC) with Aadhaar and land records.

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Fasal Bima Yojana (PMFBY)
schemes.benefit: Comprehensive insurance cover against failure of crops due to natural calamities, pests, or diseases.

eligibility.crops_covered: Food & oilseeds crops, annual commercial/horticultural crops.
eligibility.premium: 2% for Kharif, 1.5% for Rabi, 5% for commercial/horticulture.

schemes.application_process: Apply via banks, CSCs, or the PMFBY portal. In case of localized damage (heavy rain/flood), damage must be reported to the insurance company or agriculture department within 72 hours.

schemes.name: Kisan Credit Card (KCC)
schemes.benefit: Short term crop loans up to ₹3 lakh at a concessional interest rate of 4% (with prompt repayment subvention). Collateral-free loan up to ₹1.6 lakh.

eligibility.target_group: All farmers (individuals/joint borrowers), tenant farmers, oral lessees, and sharecroppers.

schemes.application_process: Submit application to a local commercial bank, RRB, or Cooperative bank along with land holding certificate and crop details.

schemes.name: State Disaster Response Fund (SDRF) / NDRF
schemes.benefit: Immediate relief for crop loss of 33% and above due to notified disasters (floods, cyclones, drought). Up to ₹17,000/hectare for rainfed areas (up to 2 hectares).
schemes.application_process: Village Administrative Officer (VAO) and Revenue Inspector will conduct a field survey (Girdawari). Submit loss details to the VAO immediately after the disaster.

schemes.name: PM-KUSUM (Solar Pump Scheme)
schemes.benefit: Up to 60% subsidy on the cost of standalone solar agriculture pumps.

eligibility.land_requirement: Must have a water source and cultivable land.

schemes.application_process: Apply through the State Nodal Agency (e.g., TEDA in Tamil Nadu) online portal.

schemes.name: Paramparagat Krishi Vikas Yojana (PKVY)
schemes.benefit: ₹50,000 per hectare for 3 years to support organic farming inputs, certification, and marketing.

eligibility.target_group: Farmers must form a cluster of 20 hectares or 50 farmers to apply.

schemes.application_process: Approach the District Agriculture Officer to form an organic farming cluster.

schemes.name: Sub-Mission on Agricultural Mechanization (SMAM)
schemes.benefit: Subsidy ranging from 40% to 80% for purchasing agricultural machinery like tractors, tillers, and harvesters.

eligibility.target_group: Small, marginal, and women farmers get higher subsidies.

schemes.application_process: Register on the Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) portal of the Agriculture Department (agrimachinery.nic.in).

schemes.name: Uzhavar Sandhai (Tamil Nadu Farmers Market)
schemes.benefit: Provides free stalls and weighing scales to farmers to sell fresh vegetables directly to consumers without middlemen.

eligibility.target_group: Must be a genuine farmer cultivating fruits/vegetables in Tamil Nadu.

schemes.application_process: Obtain an identity card from the Department of Agricultural Marketing and Agri Business, signed by the local Horticulture/Agriculture officer.
